raw_consumer_statement:
Hello, I reserved a place on XXXX between XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX XXXX XXXX weeks prior in XXXX. 
A few days after I made the reservation, I contacted the host and asked what his policy was if I brought my XXXX son. He wanted an additional {$800.00} for him to tag along which I felt was price gouging. 
I asked the renter if I could cancel my reservation, and he can rent his property to someone else and he made the following promise to me : ( see attached ) " You will need to cancel the booking, and I need you to do so immediately, to give the most amount of time for others to book the space ... My existing refund policy will remain in effect until they do - at which point I will refund you here through the app. '' Translating that the host had made a promise outside of XXXX 's refund policy. 

Prior to the rental I looked at XXXX and noticed the property was not available for others to rent and appeared to be reserved for all XXXX nights as the days were stricken from the calendar and it said unavailable when I clicked on those days. 

On the final day I contacted the host asking him for a full refund as he had the place occupied all XXXX nights per the app calendar and he told me he only had the place reserved XXXX of the XXXX nights. I asked him why the other XXXX nights were not available for others to rent and he told me he can do whatever he wants with his property. 

This was a broken promise by him as he promised to rent it to others and with him removing the listing it was a breach of our agreement. 

I contacted XXXX customer support and they told me they abide by their refund policy and acknowledged that he broke his promise and that was okay with them, they offered me a XXXX night refund which I declined. 

I then contacted my credit card AMEX, I have been a customer for nearly XXXX years. I filed a dispute and sent them everything. They said they do not disposition outside of XXXX 's cancellation policy, but they acknowledged that the host had broken a promise with me and it wasn't right. 

I contacted AMEX again today and filed a complaint as they didn't properly disposition my case and would ask the CFPB for assistance with this matter. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX
